message:
fix(raptorq): compute per-block repair counts for multi-block sends to prevent symbol-pool exhaustion

RaptorQSender::send_object previously passed a whole-object repair
count (computed from total data length) into the encoder's per-block
repair override. For multi-block payloads, this caused each block to
emit the full object's repair count rather than its proportional share,
over-emitting repair symbols by a factor equal to the number of blocks
and risking symbol-pool exhaustion.

New compute_total_repair_count function iterates blocks using the
configured max_block_size, computing per-block source symbol counts
and summing per-block repair counts. This correctly accounts for the
ceiling effect on each block's source-to-repair ratio.

The encoder call is changed from encode_with_repair (which takes an
explicit repair override) to encode (which uses the encoder's internal
repair calculation), since the pipeline now configures the encoding
config with the correct overhead and the encoder handles per-block
distribution internally.

compute_repair_count_for_source_symbols extracted as a shared helper
for both the existing single-block path and the new per-block loop.

Test added: compute_total_repair_count_uses_per_block_ceilings verifies
that a 161-byte payload with 80-byte blocks and 8-byte symbols produces
3 total repairs (1+1+1 from three blocks) instead of 2 (from treating
it as a single 21-symbol block).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
